title: Local-first spokes can offload Appwrite keepalive to a tiny Vercel sidecar
context: A spoke needed Appwrite database keepalive but the main app had to remain local for spoke-to-spoke lug delivery. Deploying the entire site to Vercel would have mixed operations concerns and exposed an unnecessary surface area.
symptom: Need reliable internet-side keepalive for Appwrite or similar hosted databases, while the main application stays local-only.
fix: Create a tiny standalone Vercel cron worker in a subdirectory. The worker pings Appwrite directly with an API key and CRON_SECRET, leaving the main app undeployed.
note: This pattern generalizes to Supabase or other hosted databases: use a narrow sidecar deployment instead of promoting the full local-first app.
